title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
On the terrace of the Imperial Hotel in Torquay, Miss Marple explains the truth behind the past crimes to Gwendolyn, Jules, and Giles. She reveals that Dr. Kennedy was a madman driven by a sinister, possessive love for his half-sister, Helen. After Helen married Kelvin Harleywood in London to escape her brother's strictness, Dr. Kennedy pretended to accept the marriage. However, when Kelvin informed his brother of the marriage, Kennedy went insane. He strangled Helen in the hallway of St. Catherine's Villa and then executed a wicked plan to frame Kelvin. Kennedy manipulated Kelvin into believing he had killed his own wife and had suffered a mental breakdown, eventually convincing him to enter a mental institution. To maintain this deception, Kennedy forged Helen’s handwriting in letters and attempted to poison Gwendolyn with brandy.

Miss Marple further explains that Kennedy murdered Lily Kimball to protect himself. He lured Lily to a meeting at the crossroads, strangled her, and replaced her original letter with a fake one to make it appear as though she were blackmailing him. This murder was triggered because a girl named Leonie had seen Kennedy digging a hole in the garden and had told Lily. Kennedy had also attempted to frame others, such as Jackie Averycliff, by manipulating perceptions of a car seen at the scene. Ultimately, Kennedy confessed his crimes to the police before dying. Gwendolyn reflects on the sinister atmosphere of the house and the tragic death of Helen, who died in her prime.
